cities.name: Savannah, GA
cities.description: Georgia's first city enchants visitors with 22 moss-draped historic squares, cobblestone streets, stunning antebellum mansions, Forsyth Park, and a thriving arts and culinary scene.

cities.name: Charleston, SC
cities.description: One of America's most beautiful cities, Charleston captivates with colorful antebellum homes on Rainbow Row, horse-drawn carriage tours, world-class dining, and the historic Battery waterfront.

cities.name: Jacksonville, FL
cities.description: Florida's largest city by area offers 22 miles of Atlantic beaches, a vibrant downtown riverfront along the St. Johns River, thriving arts districts, and year-round outdoor recreation.

cities.name: Columbia, SC
cities.description: South Carolina's capital blends state government, the University of South Carolina's campus energy, a growing food scene along the Congaree River, and rich Civil War history.

cities.name: Augusta, GA
cities.description: Host of the Masters Golf Tournament, Augusta combines world-class golf at Augusta National with a revitalized Riverwalk downtown, a growing medical district, and deep Southern charm.

cities.name: Macon, GA
cities.description: Georgia's music city is the birthplace of Otis Redding, Little Richard, and the Allman Brothers Band, paired with the ancient Ocmulgee National Monument and world-famous cherry blossom festivals.

cities.name: Columbus, GA
cities.description: Home to Fort Moore, Columbus features the world's longest urban whitewater rafting course on the Chattahoochee River, a thriving Midtown arts district, and the National Infantry Museum.

cities.name: Hilton Head Island, SC
cities.description: A barrier island gem renowned for world-class golf resorts, 12 miles of pristine Atlantic beach, the iconic Harbour Town lighthouse, and abundant water sports and nature trails.

cities.name: Wilmington, NC
cities.description: North Carolina's largest coastal city boasts a stunning riverfront historic district, three barrier island beaches, a booming film industry backdrop, and the WWII battleship USS North Carolina.

cities.name: Orlando, FL
cities.description: Beyond the theme parks, Orlando is a thriving metro hub with world-class dining, Lake Eola's scenic downtown, a booming tech industry, and easy access to the Space Coast.

cities.name: Charlotte, NC
cities.description: The Queen City is the Southeast's banking capital with Uptown's gleaming skyline, the Panthers and Hornets franchises, vibrant South End and NoDa neighborhoods, and the US National Whitewater Center.

cities.name: Atlanta, GA
cities.description: The cultural capital of the South is a cosmopolitan mega-city with world-class museums, the Georgia Aquarium, the birthplace of the civil-rights movement, top universities, and a legendary food and music scene.

cities.name: Beaufort, SC
cities.description: The jewel of the Lowcountry, Beaufort's antebellum mansions, Spanish moss-draped live oaks, Gullah Geechee cultural heritage, and waterfront setting make it one of the South's most picturesque small cities.

cities.name: Brunswick, GA
cities.description: Gateway to the Golden Isles, Brunswick is a historic port city with a charming Victorian downtown, fresh seafood straight off the dock, and easy access to Jekyll, St. Simons, and Sea islands.

cities.name: Gainesville, FL
cities.description: Home to the University of Florida and Gator Nation, Gainesville is a dynamic college city surrounded by natural springs, Paynes Prairie State Preserve, and a lively arts and live-music scene.

cities.name: Tallahassee, FL
cities.description: Florida's capital city is home to Florida State University and FAMU, the historic capitol buildings, Cascades Park, and a beautiful canopy road network of ancient oaks draped in Spanish moss.

cities.name: Athens, GA
cities.description: The Classic City is the home of the UGA Bulldogs and one of the top music towns in America—birthplace of R.E.M. and the B-52s—with a thriving downtown bar scene, eclectic eateries, and classic collegiate energy.

cities.name: Hinesville, GA
cities.description: Home to Fort Stewart, the largest Army installation east of the Mississippi, Hinesville is a welcoming military community with affordable living, growing retail, and easy access to the Georgia coast.

cities.name: Richmond Hill, GA
cities.description: One of the fastest-growing cities in Georgia, Richmond Hill offers scenic Ogeechee River access, excellent Bryan County schools, a charming downtown, and a short drive to both Savannah and the Golden Isles.

cities.name: Rincon, GA
cities.description: A rapidly expanding bedroom community in Effingham County northwest of Savannah, Rincon offers affordable new construction homes, top-rated public schools, and quiet suburban living with easy I-95 access.

cities.name: Bluffton, SC
cities.description: Tucked along the May River, Bluffton's charming Old Town district—lined with live oaks and artisan galleries—sits at the gateway to Hilton Head Island, offering authentic Lowcountry living with a small-town feel.

cities.name: Statesboro, GA
cities.description: Home to Georgia Southern University and the Eagle Nation, Statesboro is a vibrant college town with a growing local food and arts scene, surrounded by farmland just an hour from the Georgia coast.

cities.name: St. Simons Island, GA
cities.description: One of Georgia's most beloved barrier islands features a storied lighthouse, bike-friendly roads through live oak canopies, the historic Fort Frederica National Monument, and a quaint Village dining district.

cities.name: Fernandina Beach, FL
cities.description: Amelia Island's charming Victorian seaport boasts the largest concentration of Victorian-era structures in Florida, pristine beaches, a booming craft-beer scene, and a proud shrimping heritage.

cities.name: St. Augustine, FL
cities.description: The oldest continuously occupied European settlement in the U.S. enchants with Spanish Colonial architecture, the ancient Castillo de San Marcos, cobblestone St. George Street, and beautiful Atlantic beach communities.

cities.name: Valdosta, GA
cities.description: Georgia's Azalea City near the Florida border is home to Valdosta State University, Wild Adventures Theme Park, a revitalized historic downtown, and a passionate community identity around Wildcat athletics.

cities.name: Florence, SC
cities.description: A growing regional center in the Pee Dee region, Florence is known for its I-95 commerce, the McLeod Health medical campus, a vibrant arts center, and convenient access to both Columbia and the Grand Strand coast.

cities.name: Myrtle Beach, SC
cities.description: The Grand Strand stretches 60 miles of Atlantic coastline offering world-class golf, a vibrant Boardwalk, Broadway at the Beach entertainment complex, fresh seafood, and endless family and nightlife attractions.

cities.name: Fayetteville, NC
cities.description: Home to Fort Liberty (formerly Fort Bragg), the largest military installation in the world by population, Fayetteville is a patriotic community with a reborn downtown, the 82nd Airborne Division Museum, and Cape Fear River access.

cities.name: Daytona Beach, FL
cities.description: The World Center of Racing is famous for the Daytona 500, Bike Week, 23 miles of hard-packed Atlantic beach, the historic Daytona Beach Bandshell, and Daytona International Speedway.
